whatChanged: Classified fixed Build Intel inspected commits as snapshot evidence with explicit source posture and latest monitoring boundaries.
whatItDoes: Keeps GStack Build Intel inspected commit proof useful as evidence while preventing it from being treated as latest upstream monitoring truth.
whyItMatters: The May 19 deep audit found a fixed Build Intel commit baseline that could become false-green freshness proof. Snapshot metadata separates inspected evidence from current monitoring truth.
whereItLives: lib/build-intel-snapshot-baseline.js snapshot baseline constants, helpers, evaluator, dogfood, and proof commands
scripts/process-gstack-build-intel-check.mjs validates snapshot semantics for GStack proof
lib/code-quality-nightly-audit.js uses the snapshot-baseline evaluator instead of raw fixed-commit reference scanning
lib/foundation-intelligence-audit-verifier.js validates snapshot posture in the intelligence audit
lib/deep-audit-findings-closure-gate.js routes the May 19 finding to this closeout
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods stale fixed-commit latest-truth logic as rejected, proves healthy snapshot metadata clears the code-quality audit finding, and advances Current Sprint to the build closeout data-source card.
reviewNext: Continue `BUILD-CLOSEOUT-DATA-SOURCE-001` in the overnight audit-control sprint.
knownLimits: This does not build a new GitHub crawler.
This does not run live GitHub extraction.
This does not import GStack code or install GStack.
This does not auto-create backlog cards from Build Intel findings.
This does not start source/value/extraction expansion.

whatChanged: Moved the Plan Critic approval threshold behind one shared registry and wired the load-bearing approval paths to that registry.
whatItDoes: Keeps Plan Critic, approval integrity, Current Sprint pass-threshold normalization, Current Sprint store mapping, and code-quality audit drift detection aligned on one approval threshold contract.
whyItMatters: The May 19 deep audit found raw approval-threshold logic duplicated across Foundation proof paths. A shared registry prevents drift without rewriting every historical approval artifact that mentions the threshold label.
whereItLives: lib/approval-threshold-registry.js canonical threshold, helpers, evaluator, dogfood, proof commands, and next-card metadata
lib/process-plan-critic.js re-exports the registry threshold for compatibility
lib/approval-integrity.js validates approval scores through registry helpers
lib/foundation-current-sprint.js normalizes Plan Critic thresholds through registry helpers
lib/foundation-current-sprint-store.js maps DB pass thresholds through registry fallback
lib/code-quality-nightly-audit.js uses registry evaluator instead of blanket raw-literal scanning
lib/deep-audit-findings-closure-gate.js routes the May 19 finding to this closeout
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods stale local threshold logic as rejected, proves below-threshold scores fail, proves the code-quality audit no longer proposes the threshold card when registry usage is healthy, and advances Current Sprint to Build Intel snapshot baseline.
reviewNext: Continue `BUILD-INTEL-SNAPSHOT-BASELINE-001` in the overnight audit-control sprint.
knownLimits: This does not rewrite every historical approval JSON, closeout, or operator label that mentions 9.8.
This does not change the approval threshold value.
This does not redesign Plan Critic scoring.
This does not start source/value/extraction work.

systemArea: Ops Admin deal-review policy source contract
whatChanged: Moved Admin deal-review policy dates, daily limit, maturity gate, source IDs, and write boundary behind one repo-local source-contract module.
whatItDoes: Keeps the Admin runner, scheduled job metadata, and Ops UI copy aligned by reading policy values from `lib/admin-deal-policy-source-contract.js` instead of duplicating cutoff/effective-date literals in each surface.
whyItMatters: The May 19 deep audit flagged policy-date drift across the runner, job config, and UI. A source-owned policy contract lets future Ops policy changes happen in one place and gives the nightly audit a concrete invariant to enforce.
whereItLives: lib/admin-deal-policy-source-contract.js policy contract, job helpers, source evaluator, and dogfood
scripts/review-admin-deals.mjs imports Admin policy values from the contract
lib/foundation-jobs.js Admin backlog job args/summary/input builders
public/ops.js Admin backlog detail sourced from job summary
lib/code-quality-nightly-audit.js Admin policy stale-finding suppression guard
proofStatus: Focused proof dogfoods stale runner-local policy constants, hardcoded scheduled job args, and hardcoded Ops UI policy copy as rejected; code-quality audit no longer proposes the Admin policy source-contract card when runner/job/UI contract usage passes.
reviewNext: Continue `APPROVAL-THRESHOLD-REGISTRY-001` in the overnight audit-control sprint.

This does not call Google Sheets, FUB, ClickUp, or external connectors.

whatChanged: Added a bounded legacy-system audit that scans local old-system roots as sanitized evidence and routes durable patterns into current backlog/source/spec/proof truth.
whatItDoes: Uses lib/legacy-system-audit.js and process:legacy-system-audit-check to represent BCrew-Buddy, old skill library, FUB/Zahnd schema, old dashboard prototype, OpenClaw metadata-only workspace evidence, and Unchained strategy notes. Every finding is classified as promote, backlog, source_note, spec, evidence_only, duplicate, reject, or approval_bound.
whyItMatters: Steve explicitly asked that the gold from the old-system/deep-audit conversation be carded and not lost. This converts useful legacy patterns into durable routing while rejecting old-code import, old-agent execution, stale report piles, and private runtime copying.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval integrity, Plan Critic, targeted legacy root evidence, metadata-only private runtime boundary, live promoted-card destinations, dogfood rejection of missing roots/unclassified findings/unsafe imports, live backlog, Current Sprint advancement, audit artifacts, and closeout registry wiring.
reviewNext: Continue STRATEGIC-INTEL-001 using the salvage map, current atom/action-router/retrieval truth, and source-backed evidence layers. Do not restart old agent sprawl or broad extraction from old-system notes.
knownLimits: This card does not import old-system code.
This card does not run old agents, crawlers, browser automation, scheduled jobs, model calls, extraction, source-system mutation, or external writes.
This card does not copy raw private runtime content, credentials, messages, media, or OpenClaw local state into repo artifacts.
This card does not approve paid/private source access, provider/tool runtime use, credential mutation, Drive permission mutation, or Value Builder split.
Legacy notes remain evidence until promoted into live source, backlog, specs, or verifier proof.

whatChanged: Added the v1 Strategic Intelligence issue ledger and feedback event ledger, seeded from current source-backed strategy-eligible synthesized items, and updated the follow-up backlog verifier to accept the shipped ledger closeout.
whatItDoes: Uses lib/strategic-intel-loop.js and process:strategic-intel-check to create intelligence_strategic_issues and intelligence_strategic_issue_events, map strategy synthesized items into owner-scored issues, preserve source/fact/atom/chunk/synthesized-item refs, encode weekly operating targets, and keep Scoper/decision work downstream. The support verifier change keeps the historical Strategic Intelligence guard honest after STRATEGIC-INTEL-001 moves from scoped to done.
whyItMatters: Steve wants continuous strategy, not old-system report piles or a dashboard that does not create action. This gives strategic issues stable identity, scoring, lifecycle, provenance, and feedback before DECISION-008 and INTEL-SCOPER-001 build on top.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval integrity, Plan Critic, strategic issue schema, source-backed issue seeding, scoring fields, evidence refs, weekly target encoding, resolution feedback events, dogfood rejection of weak issue fixtures, live backlog rows, Current Sprint advancement, and closeout registry wiring.
reviewNext: Continue DECISION-008. It must use intelligence_strategic_issues and write resolution feedback back to the strategic issue loop instead of creating disconnected doctrine/backlog records.
knownLimits: This card does not run extraction, browser automation, old agents, provider/model calls, or old-system runtime.
This card does not build the Strategy UI or Scoper implementation.
This card does not fake resolved-to-applied outcomes; it encodes the target and measures actual status separately.
This card does not write external systems, send messages, mutate credentials, mutate provider config, or change Drive permissions.

whatChanged: Added the final Foundation-only sprint closeout gate and continuous-work readiness decision.
whatItDoes: Uses process:foundation-sprint-closeout-continuous-work-ready-check to require green System Health, green repeated-failure gate, green backlog hygiene, green Current Sprint truth, completed required sprint cards, and clean/synced main before closing the sprint. It records the recommendation that continuous Foundation Builder can run under repair-first rules, while full Value Builder split is deferred until the next clean overnight/morning cycle or explicit Steve approval.
whyItMatters: Steve needs the system to keep working without babysitting, but not by silently restarting parallel chaos. This closeout turns the end-of-sprint decision into proof-backed operating truth instead of chat memory.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates Plan Critic approval, final readiness dogfood, live System Health, repeated-failure action gate, backlog hygiene, Current Sprint truth, required-card closure, clean main, closeout registry, and no active blocker after close.
reviewNext: Start the next sprint from morning audit truth; continuous Foundation Builder is ready under repair-first rules.
knownLimits: This does not start Value Builder split by itself.
This does not approve broad private extraction, external writes, sends, permission mutation, credential mutation, paid/provider access, or browser-auth work.
This does not replace the morning raw-green audit.
This does not make historical failed job rows disappear; it requires current gates to be green and repeated failures resolved.

whatChanged: Added a local Steve-facing Foundation operator pulse API and Runtime Health panel, and tightened full diagnostics backlog payload size without dropping verifier-critical row shape.
whatItDoes: Uses /api/foundation/operator-pulse and renderFoundationOperatorPulsePanel() to summarize System Health, repeated-failure status, Current Sprint, backlog hygiene, approvals, recent builds, recent changes, and the next card from live Foundation truth. The supporting hub-read route change trims expanded whyItMatters text from full diagnostics backlog rows while preserving the dedicated backlog detail route.
whyItMatters: Steve needs the short operator answer before diagnostic detail: what is green, what needs repair, what needs approval, and what should run next. This keeps the sprint moving without hiding red health or relying on chat memory.

whatChanged: Added bounded application/ics calendar invite text extraction to the Gmail attachment lane, added calendar retry prefixes to the target budget, and made explicitly retried skipped attachment rows take priority over fresh backlog.
whatItDoes: Uses process:email-attachments-check to prove the scheduled email-attachments-backfill lane stays bounded/idempotent, supports Gmail PDF/text/calendar attachments, retires existing calendar_invite_not_in_v1 skips through a governed target run, and preserves explicit skip routing for images, Office files, spreadsheets, slides, audio, video, and other unsupported attachment classes.
whyItMatters: Email attachment extraction is useful only when safe file types become source-backed artifacts and unsafe classes stay owned follow-up work. This slice turns calendar invite skips into local artifacts without sends, external writes, credential mutation, or broad private extraction.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates Plan Critic approval, live email attachment target budget/state, application/ics extraction support, retry-skip prioritization, governed target run proof, System Health, repeated-failure gate, closeout registry, and Current Sprint advancement.

This does not implement OCR/images/audio/video/multimodal extraction.

The proof also parks Skool, Zoom audio recovery, old-system report mining, and video link inventory with owner/reason/next trigger instead of stopping the sprint.

whatChanged: Added focused current-day source freshness proof for Gmail, Missive, meeting notes, Google Calendar, and Slack. The proof reads live extraction-control and Foundation job-ledger state, verifies cadence freshness and item-level crawl proof, and requires current-day failed items to be repaired to zero when governed recovery is safe.
whatItDoes: Uses buildExtractCurrentSourceFreshnessStatus() to prove five current-day targets are active, scheduled, latest-success, fresh for their cadence, item-ledgered, and free of hidden failed items. The dogfood path rejects stale lanes, hidden partial failures, and unsafe approval-bound operations being presented as green.
whyItMatters: Backfill and richer extraction are only useful if today stays fresh. Steve can now see that the current-day source lanes are green before the system works backward through history.

whatChanged: Added the live source/connector layer model to /api/source-of-truth and rendered it on Data Sources so source contracts, connector reach, trust, freshness/drift, and dependencies are visible as separate layers.
whatItDoes: Builds compact source rows with source status, connector status, trust status, freshness status, drift status, owner, direct source links, dependent systems, and connector IDs. Connector rows carry connector status and an explicit trust boundary. Dogfood proves a working connector can support trusted and untrusted sources without upgrading trust.
whyItMatters: Source/extract work should not expand on fuzzy source truth. Steve can now tell whether a source is trusted, merely readable, technically connected, stale/watch, blocked, or depended on by a system without treating connector access as business truth.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, Plan Critic, source-layer dogfood, live /api/source-of-truth layer counts, Data Sources rendering, System Health, repeated-failure gate, closeout registry, and Current Sprint advancement.
